topics: Significance and New Trends in Micro and Nano-Scale Systems
Elements of MEMS: Microsensors and Microactuators; Microelectronics Fabrication Process: Basics of the Microelectronics Fabrication Process
Polymers and Metals Used in MEMS

Additional Materials: Silicon Compounds (SiO2, Si3N4), Polycrystalline Silicon, Gallium Arsenide, Quartz, Piezoelectric Crystals, Conductive Polymers; Packaging and Integration: Glass Encapsulation, MEMS Process Integration Strategies
Bulk Micromachining: Micro Needles, Micro Nozzles
Etching Techniques: Dry Etching, Plasma Etching, Wet Etching; High Aspect-Ratio Processes: LIGA Process
Thin Film Deposition: Chemical Vapor Deposition (CVD), Physical Vapor Deposition (PVD), Evaporation and Sputtering
Mechanical Properties of Silicon and Related Thin Films
Spring Constant and Resonant Frequency; Electrical Concepts in MEMS: Semiconductor Materials, Calculation of Charge Carrier Concentration, Conductivity and Resistivity of Semiconductors

Piezoelectric Sensors; Actuation Techniques: Thermal Forces, Shape Memory Alloys, Piezoelectric Crystals, Electrostatic Forces (Parallel Plate, Torsion Bar, Comb Drive Actuators), Micromechanical Motors and Pumps
Case Studies of MEMS: MEMS Inertial Sensors in Automobiles (Airbag Deployment, Automobile Navigation), MEMS Vibratory Gyroscopes, MEMS Accelerometers, MEMS Devices in Commercial Applications
